ORTHOPAEDIC CONSULTATION NOTE

CHIEF COMPLAINT: Left hip pain

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
72-year-old female with progressive left hip pain over 2 years. Pain is located in the
groin and lateral hip, radiating to the thigh. Pain with weight-bearing and at rest.
Unable to put on shoes or socks without assistance.

IMAGING:
AP pelvis and lateral left hip radiographs show Kellgren-Lawrence Grade IV changes
with superior joint space narrowing, large acetabular osteophytes, and femoral head
flattening consistent with end-stage degenerative joint disease.

DIAGNOSIS: Primary osteoarthritis, left hip (ICD-10: M16.12)

CONSERVATIVE TREATMENT HISTORY:
1. Acetaminophen 1000mg TID - 6 months - minimal relief
2. Physical therapy (2x/week, 6 weeks) - no improvement
3. Cortisone injection (80mg methylprednisolone) - 2 weeks relief only

RECOMMENDATION:
Recommend left total hip arthroplasty. Patient has failed conservative management
and has significant functional limitation affecting quality of life.

Planning anterior approach total hip arthroplasty with ceramic-on-polyethylene
bearing surface.
